name: More Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
Now the starters. This is the starter that Connie used. After that is an alternative for people who have trouble with the first one.
Potato starter I:
Mix the ingredients. Cover loosely and leave in a warm place, such as on top of a water heater, for 3 or 4 days. If the mixture starts to smell yeasty prior to 3 or 4 days, or is ready, go ahead and feed it and start making bread. See the feeding directions below.
Potato starter II:
2 envelopes (2 TBSP) active dry yeast
1/2 cup warm water (105-115°F)
1/2 cup warm water (105-115°F)
2/3 cup sugar
3 TBSP instant potato flakes
Dissolve the yeast in 1/2 cup warm water in a medium mixing bowl. Stir in 1 cup warm water, sugar, and potato flakes.
Let the mixture sit out all day, then refrigerate for 10 to 15 days (a 10-day schedule works fine). Remove from refrigerator and feed using the feeding instructions below. Now you are ready to use 1 cup of the starter to make bread. Return the rest of the starter to the refrigerator for another 5 to 10 days. Before making your next batch of bread, feed the starter again.
Feeding Recipe:
Starter from recipes above
1 cup Water
3/4 cup Sugar
3 TBSP instant Potato flakes
Stir ingredients well and keep at room temperature for 10 to 12 hours. Makes 2 - 3 cups.
